title: Does every 2-colored cocktail party graph admit a cover by two monochromatic diameter-2 subsets?
lean_gate_reason: Lean would only archive the already closed empty-signature-class slice. The shortest remaining path is still a combinatorial closure of the locally saturated case, so formalization is not the gate to a sealed publication packet.
family_affinity: moderate; critical-pair signature analysis may transfer only to alpha=2 hosts with rigid nonedge structure
generalization_signal: Fix a deleted pair, partition vertices by the four endpoint color signatures, and force covers unless a locally saturated critical pattern survives.
strongest_honest_claim: In every red-blue coloring of a cocktail-party graph, if some deleted matching pair has an empty signature class among RR, RB, BR, BB, then the vertex set is covered by two monochromatic diameter-2 subsets, in fact by two monochromatic stars. In addition, exhaustive search shows the 6-vertex cocktail-party graph satisfies the full conjecture under the faithful source reading that the two covering colors may coincide.
candidate_theorem_slice: Empty signature class around a deleted pair forces a two-star cover; the full conjecture is additionally verified for the 6-vertex cocktail-party graph under the source-faithful same-or-different-color reading.
paper_title_hint: Empty signature classes and the six-vertex case for two monochromatic diameter-2 covers in cocktail-party graphs
theorem_slice_target: Prove the singleton-residual strengthening: if some deleted matching pair has a signature class of size at most 1, then the graph is already coverable by two monochromatic diameter-2 subsets; equivalently, reduce the full conjecture to a clean locally saturated critical-pair theorem.
fallback_target: Preserve the exact empty-signature-class theorem slice together with the exact 6-vertex verification under the faithful same-or-different-color reading, without claiming progress on the full locally saturated case.
next_blocker: There is still no rigorous absorption lemma for locally saturated deleted pairs: the cross-edges between X_RB and X_BR do not yet force internal diameter-2 witnesses inside one of the two covering sets.
next_feeder_instances: Prove or refute the singleton-residual lemma for one deleted pair.
Classify locally saturated colorings of the 8-vertex cocktail-party graph.
next_action: Target the singleton-residual lemma first; if it stalls, classify locally saturated 8-vertex colorings and keep the current exact slice as fallback rather than overstating the full conjecture.
